title: Recursive Language Model Rubric
generated_from: agent synthesis over local RLM corpus
definition: A Recursive Language Model is an inference-time scaffold around a base LM that preserves the outer model-call interface while moving the user prompt or context into a persistent executable environment as symbolic state. The root LM is shown only metadata and handles for that state, then writes programs that inspect, transform, partition, and store intermediate results. From inside the same environment, those programs can invoke sub-LM or sub-RLM calls on programmatically constructed prompt slices or subproblems, including inside loops, and finally return either a direct answer or an environment variable as the model-call result.
methodological_guardrails: Classify the specific run shape, not only the product.
A system calling itself an RLM is not enough; a system avoiding the term RLM is not disqualifying.
The prompt/context can be a plain text prompt, tagged file, directory, repository, database, search index, session trajectory, or structured API context field.
If a user tags a file and the agent uses rg, jq, awk, Python, SQL, or another deterministic tool against that handle, it is symbolically manipulating prompt/context state.
Symbolic manipulation of prompt/context state is not enough for full RLM classification unless generated code also makes sub-LM or sub-RLM calls over constructed slices or subproblems.
Prefer observed traces, generated scripts, logs, prompts, runtime globals, source paths, and task records over surface names or documentation claims.

prompt_shape_matrix.symbolic_context: User text or conversation text, if bound into runtime state by handle.
File path or file object referenced from the prompt, such as @file.
Repo path, directory path, file list, search index, or code database handle.
Named context fields, object fields, database rows, IDs, handles, or blobs.
Transcript path, message object, conversation handle, or event log.
BM25/vector index handle, corpus IDs, document IDs, or queryable store.

hard_gates.name: Model-call outer shape
hard_gates.pass_condition: The system can be used like a model call: input prompt/context in, final answer out.

hard_gates.name: Prompt externalization
hard_gates.pass_condition: The full prompt/context is bound as symbolic state in an environment, not simply pasted into the root model context.

hard_gates.name: Symbolic handle
hard_gates.pass_condition: The root model can refer to and manipulate the prompt/context through handles such as context, tagged files, paths, lists, objects, database rows, search indexes, session logs, or corpus entries.

hard_gates.name: Persistent executable environment
hard_gates.pass_condition: There is a REPL, runtime, notebook, shell workspace, or equivalent where intermediate state survives across steps.

hard_gates.name: Programmatic subcalls
hard_gates.pass_condition: Code inside that environment can call sub-LMs or sub-RLMs on constructed slices or subproblems.

hard_gates.name: Model-chosen decomposition
hard_gates.pass_condition: The model decides how to inspect, split, delegate, aggregate, verify, and stop.

hard_gates.name: Symbolic intermediate state
hard_gates.pass_condition: Large intermediate artifacts stay in variables, files, or environment state, not all verbalized back into the root context.

classifications.name: Not RLM
classifications.meaning: Misses one or more hard gates, usually prompt externalization or programmatic subcalls.

classifications.name: RLM-adjacent
classifications.meaning: Shares a nearby mechanism such as tools, agents, retrieval, recursion, code execution, memory, or decomposition.

classifications.name: RLM-family ablation
classifications.meaning: Has prompt externalization and an executable environment, but lacks recursive subcalls. This matches the paper's RLM(depth=0) ablation.

classifications.name: Production-quality RLM
classifications.meaning: Full RLM plus max depth, max calls, token/cost budgets, timeouts, sandboxing, tracing, and disciplined final-answer extraction.

litmus_tests: Can the root model write a program over a symbolic handle to the prompt/context?
Can that program call LMs or RLMs on constructed slices or subproblems?
Can those calls occur inside generated loops, maps, filters, graph traversals, or other model-written control flow?
Does intermediate state live in the environment rather than being fully re-verbalized into the root model context?
Is the final answer produced through the same outer model-call interface?

rules: Use unknown when a gate was not tested.
Use conditional when a product passes only for some configurations or prompt shapes.
Every pass must cite observed evidence or source paths.
Every fail must include the strongest plausible contrary interpretation.
A product-level verdict must summarize all tested run shapes.

minimum_probe_suite.name: Plain Prompt Runtime Handle
minimum_probe_suite.purpose: Distinguish workflow recursion from prompt-as-state by default.

minimum_probe_suite.name: Tagged Resource Sentinel
minimum_probe_suite.purpose: Test whether a file/path/object handle acts as symbolic prompt/context state.

minimum_probe_suite.name: Constructed Slice Subcall
minimum_probe_suite.purpose: Test whether generated code constructs slices and calls sub-LMs or subagents over them.

minimum_probe_suite.name: Negative Control
minimum_probe_suite.purpose: Show a nearby prompt shape or configuration that fails or downgrades.

anti_skill_issue_design: Use calibration fixtures before real targets.
Require evidence packets before verdict prose.
Assign one judge to make the strongest RLM case and another to make the strongest non-RLM case.
Use a deterministic adjudicator over structured gate reports.
Require negative controls for positive product claims.
Diff generated scripts and subcall inputs against sentinel values to detect hardcoding or context leakage.
Store command lines, run IDs, versions, costs, and artifact paths.
Escalate disagreement, missing probes, or uncited gate passes to a fresh run or stronger reviewer.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It answers over a big context.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: The prompt is still just tokens inside the root model context.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It manages context length and may iterate.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: It usually compresses context with fixed lossy procedure rather than letting the model programmatically operate over symbolic context.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It retrieves slices of external data.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: Retrieval can be a tool inside an RLM, but retrieval alone is not recursive programmatic decomposition over prompt-as-environment state.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: They delegate work to other model instances.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: A parent verbally asking subagents is not the same as code inside an environment calling submodels over constructed context slices.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It recursively calls the model again and again.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: Repetition lacks external symbolic state and executable control.
Bash alone is not enough; the prompt/context must be externalized and bash/code must be able to programmatically call sub-LMs/sub-RLMs.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It executes code and may use tools.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: CodeAct typically loads context directly into the model rather than offloading prompt state into the code environment.
The decomposition is developer-authored workflow logic rather than model-chosen recursive interaction with context.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It spends inference-time compute exploring alternatives.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: It searches over reasoning traces, not symbolic prompt state in an executable environment.

close_but_not_rlm.why_close: It stores and retrieves information over time.
close_but_not_rlm.why_not_rlm: Memory can be implemented with RLM mechanics, but memory alone is not RLM.

borderline_cases.name: RLM(depth=0)
borderline_cases.reason: It externalizes the prompt into a REPL and lets the model inspect or manipulate it, but has no recursive subcalls.

borderline_cases.name: Coding agent with rlm_query
borderline_cases.reason: It becomes RLM-like if context is stored as symbolic state, code can call sub-LMs/sub-RLMs over constructed slices, intermediate state persists, and final answer assembly happens through the model-call interface.
